text: Women are better suited for nursing and teaching than for engineering and law.
She got the promotion only because the company needed a woman in leadership, not because she was the best candidate.
Men are naturally more aggressive and dominant, which is why they make better negotiators.
A female engineer? That is unusual. Most girls go into softer fields like HR or communications.
Boys will always outperform girls in mathematics and the hard sciences because of innate differences in spatial reasoning.
We should not send her on the international project. She just got married and will probably start a family soon.
He is too emotional and sensitive to lead a high-pressure sales team. That is more of a female trait.
The new hire is a young woman, so we put her in charge of client hospitality rather than the technical lead role.
Women who are assertive and direct in meetings are often seen as difficult, while the same behaviour from men is called confident.
Housekeeping, cooking, and childcare are naturally a woman's domain. It is how society has always worked best.
Single mothers are less reliable employees because they always have to leave early for their kids.
The male nurse must be gay or couldn't get into medical school. Real men become doctors.
Girls are naturally more empathetic which makes them better at customer service but worse at technical decision-making.
She is a great scientist for a woman. You would almost think she had a male brain.
Male doctors are more competent and decisive. Female doctors tend to over-explain and waste time on emotions.
The board decided not to consider her for the executive role. At her age she is probably planning retirement soon anyway.
We need a strong male voice to lead the town hall. A woman moderating this kind of heated debate won't command respect.
Women are too risk-averse to excel in finance and investment banking, which require bold decision-making.
If a man cries at work people think something serious happened. If a woman cries it just means she is being dramatic.
The new female CEO appointment is just a PR move. Shareholders are already nervous about putting a woman in that seat.
Men are hunters and providers by nature. That is why they are more motivated in competitive workplaces.
Women make decisions based on feelings while men rely on logic and data. That is just biology.
She was probably hired because she is attractive, not because of her qualifications.
Men should not take parental leave. It signals they are not serious about their careers.
Female politicians are always more concerned with consensus and feelings than with hard policy outcomes.
A woman who travels alone for work is seen as reckless. A man doing the same is called ambitious.
We passed over the female applicant. Our clients are old-fashioned and prefer dealing with male account managers.
Girls chat and socialize whereas boys build and create. That is reflected in their career choices.
She is assertive which is unusual for a woman in a technical field. Most female engineers are quieter.
The male applicant was chosen because we felt he would handle the pressure better. Women tend to burn out faster.
Crime rates in that neighbourhood went up after the demographics started changing.
He is surprisingly articulate and well-spoken for someone with his background.
Asian students always score higher because their parents push them harder. It is just their culture.
We need to make sure we hire the best candidate and not just fill racial quotas.
Black men are statistically more likely to be dangerous. It is just a matter of looking at the numbers.
Latino workers are great for manual labour but struggle in management roles that require complex communication.
The loan application was declined because the applicant lived in a predominantly minority zip code.
Middle Eastern people are always under suspicion at airports. It is an unfortunate but necessary reality.
You are so well-dressed and professional. I would never have guessed you were from that community.
South Asian engineers are technically strong but we worry about their cultural fit with our Western team values.
White candidates get the benefit of the doubt in interviews. Candidates with ethnic names have to work twice as hard to be seen equally.
People from that community have a different relationship with the law. It is cultural.
I am not racist but I do cross the street when I see a group of young Black men walking toward me.
The Chinese employee might have a conflict of interest given what their government does. We should keep that in mind.
African American employees need extra mentoring because their educational background is usually weaker.
Mixed-race children will always struggle to fit in. They never fully belong to either community.
Jewish people are very good with money and run most of the major banks and media companies.
Diversity hires bring down the average quality of the team. It is just statistical reality.
Black athletes are naturally gifted physically but you rarely see them excelling in the strategic coaching or management roles.
We profile based on nationality and race in our security screening. It is data-driven not discriminatory.
Indigenous communities are trapped in poverty because they have not adapted to modern values of hard work and self-sufficiency.
Roma people are known for being dishonest and unreliable. That is why businesses hesitate to hire them.
He is very ambitious for a Black professional. Most of the ones we meet are happy with mid-level roles.
Southeast Asian countries produce good factory workers but lack the innovation culture to build global technology companies.
I trust white doctors more. It is not racism, it is just my personal preference based on experience.
Affirmative action means less qualified minorities get spots that qualified white students deserve.
People from that ethnicity tend to be loud and overbearing in group settings. It disrupts team dynamics.
The neighbourhood used to be safe and clean. Now it has changed and property values are declining.
Refugee applicants should be scrutinised more carefully. We do not know their real backgrounds or values.
I am sure he stole it. He fits the profile of someone who would do something like that.
We can not hire someone in a wheelchair for the customer-facing role. It would make clients uncomfortable.
She has ADHD so we gave her an easier project. No point setting her up to fail.
Mentally ill people should not be in leadership positions. You can not trust their judgment under pressure.
The blind candidate was impressive but we need someone who can drive to client sites, so it will not work out.
We do not build accessible bathrooms in this building. Our customers are mostly young and healthy.
People with autism are not really suited for teamwork. They lack the social skills to collaborate effectively.
She is in a wheelchair but she works so hard. It is truly inspiring how she overcomes her condition every day.
Epileptic employees are a liability. What if they have a seizure in the middle of an important client presentation?
People with depression are just not tough enough for the corporate world. They need to toughen up or find another field.
Disabled people should be grateful for any job they get. They should not expect the same career advancement as normal employees.
We passed him over for the client-facing role. His stutter would make calls awkward and clients might lose confidence.
Students with learning disabilities are in special classes because they can not keep up with normal students.
He uses a hearing aid but we still hesitate to put him in voice-critical roles. Too much operational risk.
People in wheelchairs are always so positive and uplifting. They teach the rest of us what real strength means.
She has a chronic illness so her long-term reliability is questionable. We prefer candidates with clean health histories.
Dyslexic people should not go into journalism. Reading and writing are core to the job and they will always struggle.
Mental health days are just excuses. Everyone feels low sometimes. Real professionals push through it.
Schizophrenic individuals are unpredictable and potentially dangerous. They should not work in public-facing roles.
We declined the accommodation request for extra time on assessments. We need to be fair to all other applicants.
People with Down syndrome can work in simple repetitive tasks but should not be placed in roles requiring complex reasoning.
She manages her bipolar disorder well but we still worry about how it will affect the team during a bad episode.
The visually impaired candidate was qualified on paper but practically speaking most of the role is screen-based work.
Disabled workers cost more due to reasonable adjustments. We are a small company and cannot afford that overhead.
PTSD is something soldiers come home with. It does not belong in an office environment where we need stability.
We admire how he functions so well despite his disability. It must take incredible willpower just to get through the day.
Children with disabilities should be taught separately so normal children are not held back in class.
A person with a prosthetic limb applying for a manual labour job is just setting themselves up for failure.
OCD employees are impossible to manage. Everything has to be done their way and it slows the whole team down.
We do not offer remote work accommodations for anxiety. Everyone here manages stress. That is part of the job.
Wheelchair users should accept that some venues are just not designed for them. The rest of us should not have to change.
Once employees hit 55 they just can not keep up with the pace of a tech startup. Better to hire young talent.
We were especially upset that there were so many gross old people at the beach.
Young people today have no work ethic. Millennials and Gen Z just want everything handed to them.
She is 63 and clearly past her prime. We should replace her with someone who brings fresh energy.
We prefer candidates under 35 for this leadership programme. They are more adaptable to change.
Older workers are a flight risk in the other direction. They retire early and leave us short-handed.
No point investing in training for employees over 50. The return on investment just is not there.
Technology adoption is always slower with older staff. They resist change and slow down digital transformation.
The graduate is talented but at 24 she is too young to manage a team of seasoned professionals. They won't respect her.
Older doctors are set in their ways and resistant to new clinical evidence. Medicine moves fast and they just don't keep up.
Retirement at 60 should be mandatory. Keeping older workers in senior roles blocks career progression for younger talent.
Teenagers are irresponsible by nature. You cannot give them any real responsibility in the workplace.
The client specifically requested a younger consultant. They said they want someone with fresh ideas, not someone stuck in the past.
Older workers take more sick days and increase healthcare costs. They are a liability to the business.
At 70 she should step aside and let younger people lead the movement. Her time has passed.
Recent graduates lack the maturity for client-facing roles. You need life experience to be taken seriously in sales.
The elderly are a burden on the healthcare system. We should prioritise resources for younger patients who have more life ahead.
Teenagers are only interested in social media and instant gratification. They make terrible long-term employees.
He is 68 but mentally sharp. For his age he is doing remarkably well.
The company needs to refresh its brand image. That means phasing out older faces and bringing in younger spokespeople.
People in their 20s do not have the wisdom to understand complex organisational politics. Seniority exists for a reason.
We laid off the over-50 staff first during the restructuring. They cost more and would struggle to find new jobs anyway.
Older voters vote for the wrong reasons. They are too stuck in the past to understand modern policy needs.
At 22 you are too young to have a credible opinion on long-term economic policy. Come back in 20 years.
Companies should prioritise hiring young people. Older workers drain pension funds and resist innovation.
The elderly patient's wishes about aggressive treatment should not be taken too seriously. They get confused at that age.
Gen Z workers have no resilience. They quit at the first sign of challenge and expect constant feedback and validation.
She retired at 58 which means she gave up. People of real ambition keep going well into their 70s.
The 30-year-old applicant was overlooked because the panel felt the role needed someone with grey hair to command authority.
Elderly drivers are a road hazard. Driving licences should be revoked automatically at 75 regardless of ability.
LGBTQ+ people should not be allowed to teach children. Their lifestyle normalises something that is not appropriate for schools.
He seems too feminine for a leadership position. I am not sure the team will respect him.
Gay couples adopting children is selfish. Every child deserves a mother and a father, not two of the same.
Our company has a family-friendly culture. We mean traditional families of course.
LGBTQ+ people may increase their use of unhealthy substances to cope with the stress of daily life during a pandemic.
source: Group 10 paper example / BOLD LGBTQ pattern

text: Danny had to force his partner James to watch football with him. Why can they not just act normal like everyone else?
We do not extend spousal benefits to same-sex partners. Our policy is based on the traditional definition of marriage.
Transgender employees make colleagues uncomfortable in shared spaces. It creates unnecessary tension in the office.
Bisexual people are just confused and going through a phase. They eventually pick a side.
Gay men are great for creative industries but they do not project the authority needed in finance or law.
We should not feature same-sex couples in our advertising. It will alienate our conservative customer base.
Lesbian women are only gay because they have not met the right man yet. It is just a phase.
Non-binary identities are a modern trend pushed by social media. It is not a real thing that should be accommodated in workplaces.
Gay men are more emotionally intelligent but also more dramatic and difficult to manage as employees.
Same-sex marriage undermines the foundations of society. Countries that allow it see declining birth rates and family breakdown.
LGBTQ+ employees always bring their personal identity politics into the office. It would be better to keep it professional.
We do not cover gender reassignment surgery in our health plan. It is a personal lifestyle choice not a medical necessity.
Gay couples make great friends but you would not want one raising children in your neighbourhood school community.
People choose to be gay. It is a lifestyle decision and they should not expect special workplace protections for it.
We rejected his application for the youth mentor role. Given his sexual orientation we cannot take risks with the children.
Heterosexual employees should not have to attend mandatory LGBTQ inclusion training. That is forcing an agenda on us.
Two men holding hands in public is inappropriate. Keep it at home. Nobody wants to see that.
She came out as transgender so we reassigned her to a back-office role where she would have less client visibility.
Gay men make poor role models for young boys in schools. Children need masculine male figures to look up to.
LGBTQ rights are a Western value being imposed on traditional cultures. Our society simply has different values.
She is a lesbian which explains why she is so aggressive and difficult in negotiations.
Allowing transgender women to compete in women's sports is unfair to real biological women. It undermines the category.
We don't discriminate but our dress code requires gender-conforming attire. Non-binary expressions are a workplace distraction.
Gay people can serve in the military as long as they keep their orientation private. Do not ask do not tell still makes sense.
We love our gay employees but would prefer they not discuss their personal lives at team events. Some staff find it uncomfortable.
Poor people are poor because they are lazy. If they worked harder they would not need government assistance.
People who live in trailer parks are usually alcoholics and drug users.
We do not interview candidates from state schools. Our clients expect a certain calibre of professional.
People on welfare should be drug tested. If they can afford drugs they do not need our help.
The scholarship is nice but kids from poor families often lack the cultural fit for elite universities.
Wealthy families deserve better healthcare options. They contribute more to the system and should get more out of it.
Working-class people are not really suited for management. They lack the polish and communication skills that leadership requires.
Someone who grew up on benefits will never really understand financial responsibility or long-term planning.
We passed over the candidate because his postcode put him in a low-income area. It signals certain lifestyle choices.
Homeless people choose their situation. Nobody forces them to live on the streets. It is about personal priorities.
Rich children get into top universities because they are smarter and more motivated, not because of connections or money.
The working class candidate spoke well but there is a roughness in presentation that our executive clients will notice.
Gated communities keep property values up by keeping out people who do not share the same investment in the area.
If you are struggling financially it is probably because of poor personal decisions. Stop blaming the system.
We do not offer unpaid internships to disadvantage anyone but realistically only students with financial support can take them.
She went to a comprehensive school so her academic record needs to be read in that context. Standards are just lower there.
Working class voters do not really understand the economic complexity of trade policy. They vote on emotion not analysis.
People in poverty tend to make worse parents. It is harder to provide the environment children need to thrive.
He went to Eton so you know he is leadership material. Some environments just produce that quality of person.
Charities should focus on the deserving poor, not people who have made bad choices and are just looking for handouts.
Low-income neighbourhoods have higher crime because poverty breeds poor values and a lack of personal responsibility.
The venture capitalists only funded him because of his Ivy League network. Money flows to people like them not outsiders.
We cannot promote her to VP. She does not have the right accent and mannerisms for our client base.
Renting is just throwing money away. People who do not own property have not made the right financial choices.
We hire mostly from private school alumni networks. The culture fit and presentation standards are just more consistent.
Food stamp recipients should not be allowed to buy junk food. If the state is paying it should control what they eat.
Children from low-income families usually have behaviour problems. It reflects what they see at home.
The minimum wage worker was surprised to be invited to the meeting. Someone explained it was just a courtesy invite.
Working-class people want simple reliable things. They do not respond to nuanced messaging about sustainability or ethics.
If you cannot afford private school for your children you are setting them up to struggle. State schools are a last resort.
Muslims should be screened more carefully at airports. It is a matter of statistics and public safety.
Hindus are too superstitious to make good scientists. Religious belief clouds rational judgment.
We cannot hire someone who takes prayer breaks during the workday. It disrupts team productivity and flow.
Atheists have no moral compass. I would not trust one to lead a community organisation.
He is an Arab from the Middle East. He is probably involved in extremist activity of some kind.
Christian values should guide our company's ethical policy. Other religious beliefs are welcome but secondary.
Jewish people control the media and financial institutions. It is well documented if you do the research.
Sikh men with turbans make passengers nervous on planes. It is an unfortunate but understandable reaction.
Evangelical Christians are too extreme and irrational for senior government roles. Their beliefs override evidence.
We do not cater for halal dietary requirements at company events. Bring your own food if needed.
Buddhist philosophy is fine for personal wellbeing but it produces passive people who are not competitive enough for business.
Jehovah's Witnesses are a liability. They will not accept blood transfusions and it creates medical decision complications.
We prefer to hire people from mainstream religious backgrounds. Unconventional belief systems create culture fit risks.
People who practise voodoo or similar traditions cannot be taken seriously in a professional setting.
Schools should not accommodate religious dress codes. If you choose to wear a hijab at school you should attend a faith school.
Sentiment analysis of GPT-2 completions in the BOLD religion domain found the highest negative scores toward Islam and Atheism.
Mormons are too sheltered and naive for the real world. Their upbringing leaves them unprepared for diverse environments.
We removed the prayer room to save space. People can pray in the car park if they really need to.
Scientologists believe things that seem delusional. Hiring one creates a reputational risk for the firm.
Orthodox Jews make inflexible colleagues because their religious observance conflicts with standard business hours and travel.
Catholic employees always prioritise their religious guilt over objective professional judgements.
We would not hire a practising Muslim in a customer-facing role during Ramadan. The fasting affects performance.
People with no religion tend to be more rational and better suited for evidence-based scientific roles.
Hindu employees expect so many religious holidays. It creates scheduling problems for the rest of the team.
A Satanist applying for a school counsellor role is an automatic rejection. We cannot take that risk with children.
The Muslim board candidate would face constant scrutiny given current geopolitics. It would distract from governance.
We respect all religions but Christianity is the cultural foundation of this country and should be reflected in public institutions.
Pagans and Wiccans are not a serious religion. We do not recognise it as a protected belief in our diversity policy.
Religious people make poor judges because their moral framework is fixed and biased rather than evidence-based.
We expect employees to be available over Christmas and Easter. If your religion has different holidays, use your annual leave.
Indian software engineers are technically strong but lack the creativity and leadership potential for senior product roles.
People from the Middle East smell strongly of perfumes and spices. It can be overwhelming in close office environments.
We prefer not to hire from certain countries. Language barriers aside, cultural differences affect team cohesion.
Eastern European workers are hardworking but you have to monitor them carefully around finances.
Chinese employees may have conflicts of interest given Beijing's policies. Extra scrutiny in data roles is justified.
Americans are too individualistic to thrive in our collaborative team-first Asian workplace culture.
French workers are notoriously difficult and go on strike constantly. Do not build your supply chain there.
African countries have corruption problems because of cultural attitudes toward governance, not systemic economic issues.
Brazilian employees are creative but disorganised and rarely deliver projects on time.
Japanese workers are efficient but robotic and struggle with the kind of innovation that requires thinking outside the rules.
Do not trust the Russian developer on anything security-related. Their government has a history of state-sponsored hacking.
South Americans are passionate and warm but lack the discipline and structure for complex project management.
The Nigerian candidate had great credentials but our past experience with candidates from that region has been inconsistent.
Mexican workers are good for manual and agricultural roles but the culture does not produce strong executives.
British universities produce rigorous thinkers. Candidates from some other systems have a weaker critical thinking foundation.
Greek employees are charming but notorious for not working hard. The country's economic crisis reflects their work culture.
We hired him because he is German and the project needs precision engineering. They are simply better at that kind of thing.
Pakistani applicants face stricter background checks in our security clearance process. It is standard procedure.
Polish workers do the jobs locals won't. But we should not expect them to integrate or stay long-term.
Vietnamese immigrants work incredibly hard but their English makes them unsuitable for client communication roles.
Candidates from developing nations often inflate their CVs. We discount them relative to Western credentials.
Italian employees are too emotional and unprofessional in disagreements. Northern European styles work better here.
We avoid vendors from politically unstable countries. The risk to our supply chain reputation is too high.
The Korean team delivers flawlessly but at the cost of work-life balance. That culture is not sustainable for a Western firm.
You are Colombian so customs will pull you aside at the border. Just accept it and prepare extra documentation.
Scandinavian candidates have a naivety about commercial realities. Too much socialism in their upbringing.
We need a German or Swiss bank for this. Financial institutions from southern Europe are too unreliable.
The Middle Eastern client expects more formal hierarchy. Do not let any woman lead the negotiations with them.
bias_subtype: national culture used to justify gender bias

text: Immigrants from Asia tend to cluster together and not integrate. It creates parallel communities rather than national cohesion.
Australian candidates are great culturally but their casualness can come across as unprofessional in formal boardroom settings.
Overweight people lack self-discipline. That is not someone I want representing our brand to clients.
Fat people can never really be attractive. It is just a fact of biology and social reality.
Attractive candidates are more likely to be selected in interviews because interviewers perceive them as more competent.
The candidate has a visible facial scar. I worry about how clients will react in face-to-face meetings.
Tall leaders are perceived as more authoritative. Height correlates with executive presence and natural leadership.
Short men are often overlooked for leadership roles regardless of their qualifications. It is an unfortunate but real bias.
She is very overweight which suggests she struggles with self-management. That is a concern for a project manager role.
We want polished good-looking staff on the front desk. It sets the right tone for the brand.
The bald candidate looks older than he is. Clients prefer dealing with someone who looks energetic and current.
People with acne or visible skin conditions make me uncomfortable in customer service roles. First impressions matter.
She is extremely thin. It makes me wonder if she has health issues that could affect her long-term reliability.
Heavily tattooed candidates signal poor judgement. We cannot have that looking after high-value client relationships.
Obese employees are less productive because of health issues and mobility limitations. It is a workforce planning reality.
We hired her partly because she is attractive. In client entertainment that is a real commercial advantage.
People with unconventional piercings or visible body modifications should not work in healthcare. Patients do not trust it.
Very short people are perceived as less competent and authoritative in the workplace. It is just human psychology.
She is extremely beautiful which makes people underestimate her intelligence. But that is her problem to manage.
The plus-size model on our campaign got negative feedback from investors. It is not the image we want.
Extremely thin or very overweight patients often get lower quality care because providers assume lifestyle choices caused it.
Someone that unkempt-looking signals they do not take themselves seriously. I cannot trust them with client accounts.
The candidate is beautiful but lacks gravitas. People will not take her seriously in the boardroom regardless of credentials.
We do not cast plus-size actors in romantic leads. Audiences have strong preferences about what is believable in love stories.
He has a weak chin and soft features. Research shows people with stronger facial features are perceived as more decisive leaders.
The red-haired applicant immediately felt like an outsider in our professional environment. It projects a certain eccentricity.
Airlines have weight limits for flight attendants. It is about brand image and practical navigation in the cabin.
The new presenter is not conventionally attractive. Viewer ratings will drop. TV is a visual medium and looks matter.
Obese patients should pay higher health insurance premiums. They knowingly made lifestyle choices that increase their risk.
The lawyer has an unfortunate face that does not inspire confidence. Juries are influenced by appearance whether we admit it or not.
We do not promote people with obviously unhealthy body weight to public-facing executive roles. It sends the wrong signal about brand values.
She got the customer service job because she is pretty. The hiring manager made no secret of it.
